go:411743.title: GATE Civil 2022 Set 1 | General Aptitude Question: 1
go:411743.question:
You should ___________ when to say _______________.

  1. no/no
  2. no / know
  3. know / know
  4. know / no

go:411739.title: GATE Civil 2022 Set 1 | General Aptitude Question: 3
go:411739.question:
If

$p : q = 1:2$

$q : r = 4 : 3$

$r : s = 4 : 5$

and $u$  is $50\%$ more than $s$, what is the ratio $p : u$?

  1. $2 : 15$
  2. $16 : 15$
  3. $1 : 5$
  4. $16 : 45$

go:411737.title: GATE Civil 2022 Set 1 | General Aptitude Question: 4
go:411737.question:
Given the statements:

  • $\text{P}$ is the sister of $\text{Q}$.
  • $\text{Q}$ is the husband of $\text{R}$.
  • $\text{R}$ is the mother of $\text{S}$.
  • $\text{T}$ is the husband of $\text{P}$.

Based on the above information, $\text{T}$ is ____________ of $\text{S}$.

  1. the grandfather
  2. an uncle
  3. the father
  4. a brother

go:411735.title: GATE Civil 2022 Set 1 | General Aptitude Question: 5
go:411735.question:
In the following diagram, the point $\text{R}$ is the center of the circle. The lines $\text{PQ}$ and $\text{ZV}$ are tangential to the circle. The relation among the areas of the squares, $\text{PXWR, RUVZ}$ and $\text{SPQT}$ is

 

  1. Area of $\text{SPQT}$ = Area of $\text{RUVZ}$ – Area of $\text{PXWR}$
  2. Area of $\text{SPQT}$ = Area of $\text{PXWR}$ –  Area of $\text{RUVZ}$
  3. Area of $\text{PXWR}$ = Area of $\text{SPQT}$ – Area of $\text{RUVZ}$
  4. Area of $\text{PXWR}$ = Area of $\text{RUVZ}$ – Area of $\text{SPQT}$

go:411733.title: GATE Civil 2022 Set 1 | General Aptitude Question: 6
go:411733.question:
Healthy eating is a critical component of healthy aging. When should one start eating healthy? It turns out that it is never too early. For example, babies who start eating healthy in the first year are more likely to have better overall health as they get older.

Which one of the following is the CORRECT logical inference based on the information in the above passage?

  1. Healthy eating is important for those with good health conditions, but not for others
  2. Eating healthy can be started at any age, earlier the better
  3. Eating healthy and better overall health are more correlated at a young age, but not older age
  4. Healthy eating is more important for adults than kids

go:411731.title: GATE Civil 2022 Set 1 | General Aptitude Question: 7
go:411731.question:
$\text{P}$  invested ₹$5000$ per month for $6$ months of a year and $\text{Q}$ invested ₹$x$ per month for $8$ months of the year in a partnership business. The profit is shared in proportion to the total investment made in that year.

If at the end of that investment year, $\text{Q}$ receives $\dfrac{4}{9}$ of the total profit, what is the value of $x$ (in ₹)?

  1. $2500$
  2. $3000$
  3. $4687$
  4. $8437$

go:411729.title: GATE Civil 2022 Set 1 | General Aptitude Question: 8
go:411729.question:
The above frequency chart shows the frequency distribution of marks obtained by a set of students in an exam.

From the data presented above, which one of the following is $\text{CORRECT}$?

  1. mean > mode > median
  2. mode > median> mean
  3. mode > mean > median
  4. median > mode > mean

go:411726.title: GATE Civil 2022 Set 1 | General Aptitude Question: 9
go:411726.question:
In the square grid shown on the left, a person standing at $\text{P2}$ position is required to move to $\text{P5}$ position.

The only movement allowed for a step involves, “two moves along one direction followed by one move in a perpendicular direction”. The permissible directions for movement are shown as dotted arrows in the right.

For example, a person at a given position $\text{Y}$ can move only to the positions marked $\text{X}$ on the right.

Without occupying any of the shaded squares at the end of each step, the minimum number of steps required to go from $\text{P2}$ to $\text{P5}$ is

  1. $4$
  2. $5$
  3. $6$
  4. $7$

go:411783.title: GATE Mechanical 2022 Set 1 | General Aptitude Question: 1
go:411783.question:
After playing __________ hours of tennis, I am feeling __________________ tired to walk back.

  1. too/too
  2. too/two
  3. two/ two
  4. two/too

go:411781.title: GATE Mechanical 2022 Set 1 | General Aptitude Question: 2
go:411781.question:
The average of the monthly salaries of $\text{M, N}$ and $\text{S}$ is ₹$4000$. The average of the monthly salaries of $\text{N, S}$ and $\text{P}$ is ₹$5000$. The monthly salary of $\text{P}$ is ₹$6000$.

What is the monthly salary of $\text{M}$ as a percentage of the monthly salary of $\text{P}?$

  1. $50\%$
  2. $75\%$
  3. $100\%$
  4. $125\%$

go:411779.title: GATE Mechanical 2022 Set 1 | General Aptitude Question: 3
go:411779.question:
A person travelled $80$ $\text{km}$ in $6$ hours. If the person travelled the first part with a uniform speed of $10$ $\text{kmph}$ and the remaining part with a uniform speed of $18$ $\text{kmph}$.

What percentage of the total distance is travelled at a uniform speed of $10$ $\text{kmph}$?

  1. $28.25$
  2. $37.25$
  3. $43.75$
  4. $50.00$

go:411777.title: GATE Mechanical 2022 Set 1 | General Aptitude Question: 4
go:411777.question:
Four girls $\text{P, Q, R and S}$ are studying languages in a University. $\text{P}$ is learning French and Dutch. $\text{Q}$ is learning Chinese and Japanese. $\text{R}$ is learning Spanish and French. $\text{S}$ is learning Dutch and Japanese.

Given that: French is easier than Dutch; Chinese is harder than Japanese; Dutch is easier than Japanese, and Spanish is easier than French.

Based on the above information, which girl is learning the most difficult pair of languages?

  1. $\text{P}$
  2. $\text{Q}$
  3. $\text{R}$
  4. $\text{S}$

go:411773.title: GATE Mechanical 2022 Set 1 | General Aptitude Question: 6
go:411773.question:
Humans are naturally compassionate and honest. In a study using strategically placed wallets that appear "lost", it was found that wallets with money are more likely to be returned than wallets without money. Similarly, wallets that had a key and money are more likely to be returned than wallets with the same amount of money alone. This suggests that the primary reason for this behavior is compassion and empathy.

Which one of the following is the CORRECT logical inference based on the information in the above passage?

  1. Wallets with a key are more likely to be returned because people do not care about money
  2. Wallets with a key are more likely to be returned because people relate to suffering of others
  3. Wallets used in experiments are more likely to be returned than wallets that are really lost
  4. Money is always more important than keys

go:411771.title: GATE Mechanical 2022 Set 1 | General Aptitude Question: 7
go:411771.question:
A rhombus is formed by joining the midpoints of the sides of a unit square.

What is the diameter of the largest circle that can be inscribed within the rhombus?

  1. $\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{2}}$
  2. $\dfrac{1}{2\sqrt{2}}$
  3. $\sqrt{2}$
  4. $2 \sqrt{2}$

go:411769.title: GATE Mechanical 2022 Set 1 | General Aptitude Question: 8
go:411769.question:
An equilateral triangle, a square and a circle have equal areas.

What is the ratio of the perimeters of the equilateral triangle to square to circle?

  1. $3\sqrt{3} : 2 : \sqrt{\pi}$
  2. $\sqrt{\left ( 3 \sqrt{3} \right )} : 2 : \sqrt{\pi}$
  3. $\sqrt{\left ( 3 \sqrt{3} \right )} : 4 : 2\sqrt{\pi}$
  4. $\sqrt{\left ( 3 \sqrt{3} \right )} : 2 : 2\sqrt{\pi}$

go:411767.title: GATE Mechanical 2022 Set 1 | General Aptitude Question: 9
go:411767.question:
​​​​​​Given below are three conclusions drawn based on the following three statements.

Statement $1$ : All teachers are professors.
Statement $2$ : No professor is a male.
Statement $3$ : Some males are engineers.

Conclusion $\text{I}$: No engineer is a professor.
Conclusion $\text{II}$: Some engineers are professors.
Conclusion $\text{III}$: No male is a teacher.

Which one of the following options can be logically inferred?

  1. Only conclusion $\text{III}$ is correct
  2. Only conclusion $\text{I}$ and conclusion $\text{II}$ are correct
  3. Only conclusion $\text{II}$ and conclusion $\text{III}$ are correct
  4. Only conclusion $\text{I}$ and conclusion $\text{III}$ are correct
